pillar.title: Best Free Chrome Screen Recorder Extension (No Watermark, No Account) — 2026
pillar.rationale: SnapRec's core commercial cluster has no pillar. The homepage is a product landing page, not an SEO document. All five existing C1 spokes are orphaned. Competitors Loom, Screencastify, and Vmaker hold positions 1-3 for 'chrome screen recorder'. This single pillar is the highest-value content investment on the site.

clusters.name: Alternatives & Comparisons Cluster

pillar.title: 5 Best Free Loom Alternatives in 2026 — No Watermarks, No Limits
pillar.status: EXISTS but THIN — needs expansion to 2800+ words, structured comparison table, and upgrade to true pillar with bidirectional spoke links
pillar.rationale: Loom alternative searches are high-intent switcher traffic. Loom's free plan limits (25 videos, 5-min cap) drive significant comparison searches. Kommodo, Supademo, and Glitter rank here with 2000-3500 word posts. SnapRec's existing post needs to become a true pillar with a feature comparison table, Loom pricing section, and proper spoke network.

gap_spokes.title: SnapRec vs Screenity — Which Free Chrome Recorder Is Better? (2026)
gap_spokes.rationale: Screenity appears in top-10 SERP results for 6 of 10 screen-recorder searches conducted. It is the primary open-source alternative to SnapRec and a common comparison target. A direct versus page captures brand-aware comparison traffic and builds topical authority in the Chrome recorder category. Overlap to C4 pillar estimated at 6/10.

gap_spokes.title: How to Use Screen Recording for Customer Support (Free Guide 2026)
gap_spokes.primary_keyword: screen recorder for customer support teams
gap_spokes.rationale: Async video for customer support is a fast-growing segment. Zight, Screendesk, and Loom target this keyword aggressively. SnapRec has no content here despite the product being a direct fit (no-account links ideal for support teams). Estimated SERP overlap to C5 pillar: 5/10.

gap_spokes.title: Async Video Messaging vs Live Meetings — Why Teams Are Switching (2026)
gap_spokes.rationale: Async video messaging is an emerging category. Loom built its brand on this concept. Teams cut 40-60% of meetings with async video (per Zight research). This top-of-funnel educational piece captures early-intent traffic that converts to extension installs. Positions SnapRec in the async work communication category, not just the 'screen recorder' commodity space.

top_3_clusters_focus.note: Per the brief: detailed hub-and-spoke architecture for the top 3 topic clusters by strategic value

cluster_1_C1.name: Chrome Screen Recorder — Core Product
cluster_1_C1.why_top_3: Highest commercial intent. Directly drives extension installs. SnapRec's core value proposition. No pillar exists — biggest single gap.

cluster_2_C2.name: Meeting & Presentation Recording
cluster_2_C2.why_top_3: Six high-volume informational posts exist with no pillar. Meeting recording is searched by tens of thousands monthly. tldv and easeus dominate with hub pages SnapRec doesn't have.

cluster_3_C5.name: Professional Use-Cases
cluster_3_C5.why_top_3: Six use-case spokes exist but are orphaned. B2B professional intent is where Loom is most vulnerable post-Atlassian acquisition. This pillar directly competes in the async work video category and supports upsell to any future paid plan.

missing_high_priority.action: Create 3200-word pillar. Include: feature comparison table (SnapRec vs Loom vs Vmaker vs ScreenApp vs Screencastify), use-case sections, FAQ schema, CTA to Chrome Web Store. Link to all 7 C1 spokes.

missing_high_priority.gap: C2 Pillar — Meeting recording umbrella hub
missing_high_priority.action: Create 3000-word pillar with jump links to Zoom, Teams, Meet, and Slides sections. Add comparison table of native recording limits. Link to all 6 C2 spokes.
Create 3500-word pillar with sections for developers, teachers, sales, support, and remote teams. Target async video category positioning. Link to all 8 C5 spokes.

missing_high_priority.gap: C1 Spoke — Screen recorder no account required
missing_high_priority.action: Create 1400-word comparison post. Feature SnapRec prominently as the top no-login option. Compare Loom (requires account), Screencastify (Google sign-in required), ScreenApp (no login needed but cloud storage).

missing_high_priority.gap: C3 Pillar upgrade from thin existing post
missing_high_priority.action: Expand from ~1000 to 2800+ words. Add structured spoke link table, comparison matrix (GoFullPage vs SnapRec vs Awesome Screenshot vs Nimbus), use-case grid, FAQ schema.

missing_high_priority.gap: C4 Pillar upgrade from thin existing post
missing_high_priority.action: Expand from ~1200 to 2800+ words. Add Loom pricing section (25 videos, 5-min cap — this is the conversion trigger), feature comparison table, and structured vs-page spoke network.

missing_high_priority.gap: C5 Spoke — Customer support use case
missing_high_priority.action: Create 1500-word guide targeting support teams. Cover: ticket resolution with video, async reply to customers, onboarding walkthroughs. CTA: SnapRec instant share links are ideal for pasting into Zendesk/Intercom.

legend.mandatory: Every spoke links to its pillar; every pillar links to every spoke. Bidirectional.
legend.recommended: Within-cluster spoke-to-spoke where SERP overlap >= 4. Strong signal.
legend.optional: Cross-cluster where SERP overlap = 2-3. Use natural anchor text.

risks.action: Differentiate in H1 and meta: post_a is 'record screen WITH SnapRec extension installed'; post_b is 'record WITHOUT installing any software' (browser-native). Keep H1 angles distinct. Both link to C1 pillar.

Teachers post = Commercial intent (tool comparison for educators buying/choosing). Online classes post = Informational (how-to for educators delivering lessons). Ensure each maintains its intent frame. No keyword conflict.

No-watermark post = broad 'any platform' tool roundup (C1 cluster). Loom alternatives = specifically Loom switchers (C4 cluster). Include a cross-link between them but ensure SnapRec is positioned differently in each (standalone recorder vs Loom replacement).
Presentation-with-webcam = presentation context (slides, talking head). Webcam+screen = general use. Keep distinct by focusing presentation post on slide deck workflows.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: Best Free Chrome Screen Recorder Extension (No Watermark, No Account) — 2026
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: SnapRec's core commercial keyword cluster has no pillar. This is the single most impactful content gap on the site. All 6 existing C1 spokes are orphaned without it.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: How to Record Any Meeting for Free in 2026 (Zoom, Teams, Meet & More)
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: Six high-volume meeting-recording posts exist without a pillar. tldv.io ranks for all six keywords with a single hub page. Creating this pillar could consolidate and multiply SnapRec's authority across all six existing posts.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: Screen Recording for Work: The Complete Guide for Teams in 2026
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: B2B professional use-case cluster has six orphaned spokes. Targets async video category where Loom is most vulnerable. Highest-intent audience for future paid plan conversion.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: Screen Recorder No Account Required — Best Free Options in 2026
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: No-login is SnapRec's #1 differentiator vs Loom and Screencastify. This keyword has strong commercial intent and SnapRec is the obvious featured tool. High conversion probability.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: How to Use Screen Recording for Customer Support (Free Guide 2026)
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: Async support video is a Loom stronghold. Support teams are high-value users (power users, share recordings externally, likely to recommend to others). Zight and Screendesk target this heavily — SnapRec has zero presence here.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: SnapRec vs Screenity — Which Free Chrome Recorder Is Better? (2026)
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: Screenity appeared in top-10 SERP results for 6 of 10 screen-recorder searches. It is the most prominent open-source alternative and a frequent comparison target. A direct vs-page captures high-conversion brand-aware traffic.

top_7_missing_content_pieces.title: Async Video Messaging vs Live Meetings — Why Teams Are Switching (2026)
top_7_missing_content_pieces.why_high_value: Top-of-funnel category education piece. Positions SnapRec in the async work communication space (not just 'screen recorder'). Teams that adopt async video are high-retention users. This is the type of content Loom uses to own the category narrative.

Informational.cta_style: Soft CTA — 'Try this with SnapRec' inline, not aggressive modal

Commercial.cta_style: Hard CTA — 'Add to Chrome (Free)' button with feature highlight, positioned at top and after comparison table

Transactional.note: No transactional content in blog — appropriate for a free product. The homepage serves this role.

Navigational.note: Excluded from clustering. 2 navigational keywords removed (Chrome Web Store listing, snaprec download).

validation.serp_overlap_supports_groupings: All spoke-to-pillar overlap scores are >= 4. Cross-cluster links are limited to pairs with overlap 2-3.
